edits.note: WP-05.1 segmented control: inactive = ink-muted on surface-overlay with a ghost border-strong outline, 6px radius. position:relative reserves the stacking context for the active inset-ring/left-bar non-color mark added on .gam-mc-dur-active.
WP-05.1 hover lifts toward ink + an accent-line edge (chrome amber, never a verdict).

WP-05.1 fixes WCAG 1.4.1 (was color-only solid-red fill). Active = danger-soft fill + danger text + 1px danger border + a NON-COLOR active mark: 3px inset danger left-bar AND an octagon glyph (U+2B23) top-right. Survives grayscale; readable pre-click since JS pre-marks [data-v="1"] (selectDuration(1)).
WP-05.1 keep active danger text on hover (the fill stays soft now, so on-accent-light white would be invisible).

WP-05.2 severity-soft bg + a colored 3px left keyline (border-left-width:3px on the severity-colored border) so every banner state reads as a structured banner. info color moves off legacy C.ACCENT(#4A9EFF) onto the WCAG-bumped info token #7cb8ff. Combined into one edit because the 5 rules are contiguous and the base rule now carries the shared keyline.

WP-05.3 custom-history (130px well): accent-line thin scrollbar (firefox + webkit) + a 14px bottom fade-mask so overflow is glanceable. Hover routes onto accent (amber chrome). Added :focus-visible offset ring; hist items are keyboard-clickable so the ring must be present. Item hex (C.BG3/C.BORDER/C.TEXT2/C.ACCENT) routed to tokens. The hover color migrates off legacy C.ACCENT to the canonical accent token.

WP-05.2 give the status container a default banner shell class so even a bare innerHTML write (or empty state) reads as a structured slot. The id is LOCKED and preserved; only an additive class is appended. The class rule is defined in the appended CSS edit below.

WP-05.2 + WP-05.5 (appended after the contiguous .gam-mc-actions block, a stable ban-tab anchor): (a) .gam-mc-ban-status:not(:empty) gives the LOCKED #mc-ban-status a structured banner shell that nests the injected .gam-mc-banner and collapses when empty so no orphan box appears pre-action; (b) #mc-ban-go gains a danger-soft well + danger ring so the confirm-ban button outweighs Cancel. Both purely additive CSS; no selector renamed.

edits.newString: id="mc-ban-unban" style="color:' + GAM_TOK.success + ';text-decoration:underline;cursor:pointer;background:transparent;border:none;padding:0;font:inherit">already banned — unban instead
edits.note: WP-05.6 ghost unban link hex #44dd66 -> GAM_TOK.success (canonical verified-green). This is inside a template literal building root.innerHTML, so the string is broken to splice GAM_TOK.success via concatenation (no var() in inline). textContent 'already banned — unban instead' (the accessible name) is preserved verbatim; id mc-ban-unban locked.

edits.newString: <div id="mc-ban-summary-preview" style="background:' + GAM_TOK.surfaceSunken + ';border:1px solid ' + GAM_TOK.border + ';border-left:3px solid ' + GAM_TOK.special + ';padding:6px 8px;font-size:11px;color:' + GAM_TOK.ink + ';line-height:1.4">
edits.note: WP-05.4 AI-summary preview well routed to tokens (surfaceSunken bg, border, ink text). Added a 3px special(purple) left keyline -- this is the AI/auto-queue lane per severity language 1.7. Dropped the ui-monospace stack (cross-font consistency). Inside a template literal so spliced via GAM_TOK concatenation.

WP-05.4 editing/saved state on the summary textarea: surfaceSunken well + tokenized border, with an amber(accent) 'editing' left-accent that flips to success(green) on blur once a real summary exists. Dropped ui-monospace. The textarea id mc-ban-summary-edit is set immediately after on the same element (locked, untouched). cssText is plain string concat per the inline channel rule (no var()).

edits.oldString: label style="font-size:10px;color:#9b9892;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em">\u{1F916} AI summary (auto-appended to notes after BAN)</label>
edits.newString: label style="font-size:10px;color:' + GAM_TOK.inkMuted + ';text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em">\u{1F916} AI summary (auto-appended to notes after BAN)</label>
edits.note: WP-05 instruction text hex #9b9892 -> GAM_TOK.inkMuted (all tertiary/instruction text routes to ink-muted per token spec 1.1). Inside the innerHTML template literal -> spliced via concatenation. Accessible label text preserved verbatim.
WP-05 hex #666 -> GAM_TOK.inkFaint (de-emphasized 'manage in popup' meta hint). Inside the innerHTML template literal -> spliced via concatenation.

summary: WP-05 ban-tab: migrates every ban-tab-unique hardcoded hex to GAM_TOK (inline cssText/innerHTML via string concat) and var(--gam-tok-*) (style rules). 14 edits. (1) Duration buttons become a real segmented control on surface-overlay with a ghost border-strong outline; the active state is rebuilt off the old solid color-only red fill into danger-soft + danger text/border + a NON-COLOR active mark (3px inset danger left-bar + octagon glyph), fixing WCAG 1.4.1 -- and JS already pre-marks the default [data-v=1] so the active duration is readable pre-click. (2) The 4 .gam-mc-banner variants gain a severity-colored 3px left keyline + token soft fills, and #mc-ban-status gets an additive .gam-mc-ban-status shell rule (:not(:empty)) so even a bare innerHTML reads as a structured banner while collapsing to nothing when empty. (3) The 130px custom-history well gets an accent-line thin scrollbar (webkit+firefox) + a bottom fade-mask + a focus-visible offset ring, with item colors tokenized. (4) The AI ban-summary preview + dynamically-created #mc-ban-summary-edit textarea move to surface-sunken + tokens with a special/amber editing left-accent that flips to success(green) on blur once a real summary exists. (5) #mc-ban-go gains a danger-soft well so confirm-ban outweighs Cancel. (6) The unban ghost link's #44dd66 routes to GAM_TOK.success. All locked selectors and accessible-name textContent preserved; the shared AI-reply-card hex (ghost/card/body/fail) was deliberately excluded because those exact strings are NON-UNIQUE (duplicated in the intel tab / WP-06 shared path) and must be migrated by the shared-component owner to stay consistent.
